title: Register signal tools' descriptors with the sidecar (not fixed builtins list)
description: spawn_cline_ctx (eval.rs:2751) calls register_tools(built_in_tool_descriptors()) — a FIXED builtins-only list (tools/mod.rs:94 builds a fresh default_with_builtins). The configured Nansen/Elfa tools are servable by the dispatch ToolRegistry but their DESCRIPTORS are never advertised to the sidecar on any path, so the model has no schema and can't call them. The optimizer over-advertise was a symptom. Fix: register the run registry's own descriptors (tools.all_descriptors() + submit pseudo-descriptors). This also keeps the optimizer (default_with_builtins) to builtins — optimizer is backtest-only and does not need signal tools (operator decision).
acceptance_criteria: Main eval path with an enabled Nansen DataToolEntry advertises the 3 Nansen descriptors to the sidecar (model has schemas). Optimizer (default_with_builtins) advertises only builtins. Test asserts the descriptor set includes registered signal tools.
close_reason: fixed in afeb5205 (verified: engine lib 1596/0, data green, cli+dashboard compile)

title: optimizer: live session state on start + IPC socket into optimize run (GH #968)
description: optimize run writes nothing to autooptimizer_session_state while live, so xvn optimize ls and the dashboard /optimizer page show nothing. Write state=running row at cycle start (before AI calls), update per phase, write terminal state on finish/cancel/kill. Expose --ipc-socket on optimize run (currently only mutate-once has it); auto-connect to /tmp/xvn-optimizer.sock when dashboard serve is running so CLI cycles appear LIVE in the SSE stream via the existing ipc.rs bridge.
close_reason: Implemented in PR: optimize run drives engine run_session loop (--max-cycles), removed run-cycle/mutate-once/demo, hid --mock, heartbeat-based stale-lock auto-clear, live session-state + --ipc-socket dashboard streaming. Unified allowlist to 'optimize run'.

title: optimizer: remove mutate-once + demo/--mock from public CLI (GH #966)
description: mutate-once exposes raw blob hashes + bypasses gating/honesty/session-state; demo/--mock runs canned fixtures with no signal value. Both imply operators should drive the cycle step-by-step, which is wrong. Remove mutate-once and demo subcommands and the --mock flag from optimize run. Move the only thing worth keeping (--ipc-socket streaming) onto optimize run itself (see #968).
close_reason: Implemented in PR: optimize run drives engine run_session loop (--max-cycles), removed run-cycle/mutate-once/demo, hid --mock, heartbeat-based stale-lock auto-clear, live session-state + --ipc-socket dashboard streaming. Unified allowlist to 'optimize run'.

title: optimizer: auto-clear stale lock on startup; drop manual unlock requirement (GH #967)
description: Killed optimize run (SIGKILL/OOM/container restart) leaves optimizer_cycle_lock set; next run exits 2 until manual xvn optimize unlock. Dead-PID + 2h backstop already exist in run_lock.rs but aren't enough for the normal kill-restart loop. Add a heartbeat column updated each cycle phase + a short stale window (configurable, ~30s) so startup auto-clears a heartbeat-dead lock, emitting stale_lock_cleared. Keep unlock for the explicit foreign-host case.
close_reason: Implemented in PR: optimize run drives engine run_session loop (--max-cycles), removed run-cycle/mutate-once/demo, hid --mock, heartbeat-based stale-lock auto-clear, live session-state + --ipc-socket dashboard streaming. Unified allowlist to 'optimize run'.

title: optimizer: continuous-loop mode + remove run-cycle alias (GH #965)
description: xvn optimize run exits after ONE cycle; run-cycle is a confusing alias implying manual looping. Wire run_cycle_cmd into the engine's existing run_session loop (session.rs: once/n_experiments/until_budget). Add --max-cycles N (0=unlimited, run until stopped). Remove run-cycle visible_alias. On SIGTERM/SIGINT: seal current cycle, release lock, write terminal session state, exit 0. Emit converged event on no-improvement convergence.
notes: Decisions: default=once (--max-cycles 0=unlimited, N=N); hide --mock (keep CI); direct TDD. Wiring run_cycle_cmd into engine run_session loop.
close_reason: Implemented in PR: optimize run drives engine run_session loop (--max-cycles), removed run-cycle/mutate-once/demo, hid --mock, heartbeat-based stale-lock auto-clear, live session-state + --ipc-socket dashboard streaming. Unified allowlist to 'optimize run'.

title: optimizer CLI/UI unification — one verb, continuous loop, live in dashboard
description: Umbrella for GH #965-#968. Unite xvn optimize CLI with the dashboard so CLI runs use the same engine path, loop continuously until stopped, auto-recover from stale locks, write live session state, and stream into the dashboard via the existing IPC socket. Goal: operators (and agents) have exactly ONE verb — xvn optimize run — that behaves identically to the UI Run button. Remove manual-control verbs that confuse agents and burn tokens.
close_reason: All four child issues (GH #965-#968) implemented and tested on branch optimizer/unify-cli-loop.

title: Fix /api/scenarios 400 on single-value tags/exclude_tags params
description:
The /api/scenarios list endpoint returns HTTP 400 when tags or exclude_tags contains exactly one value. serde_urlencoded (used by axum Query extractor) fails to deserialize a single-occurrence key (?tags=a) into Vec<String> — it expects either repeated keys (?tags=a&tags=b) or nothing.

Root cause: ListParams uses Vec<String> for tags/exclude_tags without a deserializer that handles the single-value case.

Fix: Add a string_or_vec custom deserializer in crates/xvision-dashboard/src/routes/scenarios.rs that accepts both a bare string and a sequence, and annotate both fields with #[serde(default, deserialize_with = 'string_or_vec')].

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /api/scenarios?exclude_tags=source:autooptimizer returns 200
- GET /api/scenarios?tags=source:autooptimizer returns 200
- GET /api/scenarios?tags=a&tags=b still works (two values)
- GET /api/scenarios (no tag params) still returns 200
close_reason: Fixed in PR #854: string_or_vec deserializer added to ListParams.tags and ListParams.exclude_tags

title: Fix: api_key_set=false for no-auth providers (ollama/llama-cpp/vllm)

Fix: replace the manual api_key_set computation with entry_has_key(entry, secrets). Add parity test for the Ollama no-auth case.
acceptance_criteria: api_key_set=true for ollama provider with empty api_key_env in /api/settings/providers response; parity test covers the no-auth case
close_reason: Fixed: row_from_entry now uses entry_has_key() — api_key_set=true for Ollama/LlamaCpp/Vllm with empty api_key_env. Added parity test. All 7 backend tests + 8 frontend tests pass.

SUSPECTS (frontend/web/src/routes/scenarios-detail.tsx:437-438,474,496-507,915-924): (1) the granularity <select> is only rendered when chart.data is truthy (:474) — with no cached bars the chart/empty-state area may show as the blank rectangle; (2) scenarioGranularityToCli (components/scenario/useBarsFetchJob.ts:106-119) passes unknown granularity values through unchanged, so a legacy/backend value not matching any CHART_GRANULARITY_OPTIONS leaves the select with no selected option and appears blank. NEEDS REPRO to confirm which (open a scenario with no bars vs a legacy granularity).
close_reason: FIXED + verified. Commit 67a0e442. scenarioGranularityToCli now maps Minute1/5/15+Week1 to CLI form (1m/5m/15m/1w) so the indicator-timeframe <select> always has a matching option. Helper wired at scenarios-detail.tsx:931. Test useBarsFetchJob.test.ts (47 pass). Parity: frontend presentation bead — live-runtime path is the rendered component itself; no backtest/eval logic duplicated, so eval-parity is N/A. Regression guard: new test fails if the fix reverts (adversarial-confirmed strong). Adversarial review: PASS (cause-b confirmed; cause-a hides control, not blank). Advisory: no component-level select-render test (pre-existing coverage gap).

title: Strategy page: 'Clear filter' returns http_error (DELETE route not registered)
description: Clicking 'Clear filter' on a strategy returns 'http_error: DSL reference: see docs/operator/filters.md'. ROOT CAUSE: frontend clearStrategyFilter() sends DELETE /api/strategy/:id/filter (frontend/web/src/api/strategies.ts:368-373, button at components/strategy/FilterCard.tsx:80-95). Backend only registers PUT on that path (crates/xvision-dashboard/src/server.rs:553); no delete_filter handler exists in routes/strategies.rs (only put_filter at :490). DELETE hits 405/validation and surfaces the DSL-reference error. FIX: register .delete(delete_filter) and add a handler that clears the filter (or have the frontend PUT an explicit null/empty-clear payload the validator accepts).
close_reason: FIXED + verified. Commit 926484c6. Registered .delete(delete_filter) on /api/strategy/:id/filter -> engine clear_strategy_filter (was PUT-only, DELETE 405'd into the DSL-reference error). LIVE PATH: frontend clearStrategyFilter() DELETE now reaches the handler; returns 204. Parity: config route, eval N/A. Test: delete_filter clears filter+activation_mode, no-op when none, validates-after-clear — independently re-run pass.

title: Strategies list: Columns toggle hides header but not body cells
description: On the Strategies page, toggling a column off via the 'Columns' button removes the column's <th> header but the <td> cells still render — the column data stays visible, only the title disappears. ROOT CAUSE: ListCard computes visibleColumns and applies it to <thead> only (frontend/web/src/components/lists/ListCard.tsx:70-74, ~234). The caller-supplied row renderer DesktopRow in frontend/web/src/routes/strategies.tsx:415-511 hardcodes all 7 <td> cells with no reference to columnState/visibleKeys. FIX: thread the visible-column set into DesktopRow and conditionally render each <td>.
close_reason: FIXED + verified. Commit 0bc34faa on qa/frontend-bugs. ListCard.renderRow threads visibleKeys Set; DesktopRow guards each <td>. Test: ListCard + strategies.test toggle a column off and assert the body cell is gone (28 pass). Parity: frontend presentation bead — live-runtime path is the rendered component itself; no backtest/eval logic duplicated, so eval-parity is N/A. Regression guard: new test fails if the fix reverts (adversarial-confirmed strong). Adversarial review: PASS (runtime reach solid, test strong). Full FE suite 2287 pass, tsc clean.

title: Optimizer B22: handle MAX_WINDOW_DAYS=120 as a breaking change for existing autooptimizer.toml
description: B22 (PR #902) added a hard cap of MAX_WINDOW_DAYS=120 in AutoOptimizerConfig::validate() covering day_window, baseline_untouched_window, and regime windows. Any existing operator autooptimizer.toml with a >120-day window now fails validation (this is the OOM-prone span the cap intentionally blocks). Decide: (a) document in MANUAL.md / dashboard wiki + add a migration note, and/or (b) make the cap a named config field so power users can opt into larger windows with explicit acknowledgement. Shipped example + seed templates were already narrowed to ~90 days in PR #902.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #978: max_window_days Option<i64> opt-in override of the MAX_WINDOW_DAYS=120 cap (day_window/baseline_untouched_window only); validate() gives an actionable over-cap message pointing at the override; MANUAL.md migration note + new autooptimizer-config wiki page. TDD; engine lib 1355 passed.

title: Optimizer B17/B6: extend kind-rotation + filter domain-constraint hints to the tournament path
description: B6 (per-retry mutation-kind rotation) and B17 (filter-path domain-constraint hints, e.g. window ops >=1, within_pct >0) were fixed only on the non-tournament Mutator::propose path. When config.tournament_enabled=true the cycle uses tournament.rs (build_proposal_user) which constructs its own prompt and never reaches build_user_payload, so neither fix applies there. Mirror both fixes into the tournament proposer. Source: PR #902 wiring verification (B17 verdict=partial).
close_reason: Implemented in PR #978: B6 kind-rotation + B17 filter domain-constraint hints mirrored into the tournament proposer (tournament.rs) by extracting shared pub(crate) helpers (annotated_filter_paths_section, kind_focus_directive) in mutator.rs; each tournament candidate writer gets a distinct rotation slot. TDD; engine lib 1355 passed.

title: fix(autooptimizer): filter not rendered in program_view markdown, causing stale_filter_baseline rejections
description: program_view::to_markdown renders Manifest, Agents, Mechanical params, Risk config — but NOT the Filter. The LLM sees filter paths via the filter_section in the mutator prompt but cannot see the filter's full current state in the main program view. This causes the LLM to propose wrong 'before' values (especially for max_wakeups_per_day when null), triggering stale_filter_baseline validation errors. Fix: add a Filter section to to_markdown_with_resolved_prompts (informational only; from_markdown already clones base.filter and doesn't need to change).
close_reason: Closed

title: Trader prompt/risk-config desync: prompt text embeds stale risk params (8.0 vs actual 2.0)

New test pins live params flow into the seed under Raw/Oracle/Causal. comments.author: latentwill
comments.text: DECISION (2026-06-16): keep the static signal_asset_identity seed as the SINGLE active source of on-chain identity for now. Reading from AssetEntry.chain/contract_address / RegistryEntry would require wiring the asset_registry OnceLock startup path (register() has zero callers today) — that is out of scope for this PR (it's the deferred registry-wiring work). title: CostRollupStrip: scale daily cap to the window for multi-day cap-state alarm
description: The cost rollup's 'this week' (trailing 7d) and multi-day 'since last here' windows compare CUMULATIVE spend against the DAILY budget cap, so capState flags 'over' once cumulative spend >= one day's cap (e.g. $40/day under a $50/day cap reads 'over' after ~1.25 days). Numerator (multi-day spend) and denominator (one-day cap) are different periods. Fix: scale the cap to the window length (cap × days-elapsed) for capState + the denominator, OR drop the alarm on multi-day windows and keep the cap as a reference only. Shipped as-is per the approved plan (line 159); tracked for refinement. Deferred from 8wn (PR #995).

title: rustfmt drift: main isn't fmt-clean + pre-commit hook not wired
description: Recurring uncommitted cargo-fmt noise in the main checkout. Root causes: (1) core.hooksPath=.git/hooks (default) and .git/hooks/pre-commit is ABSENT — the tracked rustfmt guard at .githooks/pre-commit never runs because scripts/setup-hooks.sh was not applied/was reset. (2) origin/main is NOT rustfmt-clean (max_width=110): committed code holds expanded forms rustfmt wants collapsed, so any 'cargo fmt' re-surfaces drift as WIP; unformatted code merges via GitHub/agents bypassing the local guard. Fix (3 steps): (a) run scripts/setup-hooks.sh in each checkout to set core.hooksPath=.githooks; (b) one 'cargo fmt --all' format-the-world commit to clean the baseline; (c) add 'cargo fmt --all --check' as a CI gate on PRs (only thing that stops unformatted merges). Toolchain pin (rust-toolchain.toml 1.95.0, rustfmt 1.9.0-stable) is consistent — NOT a version mismatch. Owner: operator, after image deploy.
notes: PARTIAL as of 2026-06-13: (b) format-the-world DONE (92cd892e 'style: full cargo fmt pass'); pre-commit rustfmt guard present (99c8d1e1, .githooks/pre-commit). REMAINING: (a) per-checkout activation via scripts/setup-hooks.sh (core.hooksPath=.githooks — local env, not a PR); (c) CI gate 'cargo fmt --all --check' on PRs (UNVERIFIED — the only thing that stops unformatted GitHub merges). notes: AUDIT 2026-06-12: ran 'cargo test -p xvision-dashboard --test eval_runs_retry' in isolation — 6 passed. Consistent with the bead (passes isolated); the concurrent full-suite failure mode was NOT reproduced by a single-binary run, so this is not a fix. Still open.
close_reason: FIXED + verified. Commit 004b76f5. Root cause: seed_launchable_strategy opened a 2nd un-WAL SQLite pool to the same file, racing AppState's pool under the concurrent suite. boot() now returns AppState; seed reuses state.pool — dual-pool race eliminated. Test-isolation bead (no eval/live parity dimension). Independently re-ran eval_runs_retry 6/6 pass; full -p xvision-dashboard 40 binaries pass.

title: Optimizer: allow structural filter mutation (add/create a filter) when base.filter is None
description: QA 2026-06-08: filter mutation only applies when base.filter.is_some() (the typed AST is walked for numeric thresholds). A strategy with no filter that makes too few/many trades cannot be helped by the optimizer's filter lever. Follow-up: let the experiment-writer ADD a filter (structural mutation), not just tune an existing one. Bigger scope than xvision-avg (which fixes lever rotation for existing filters/prompts).
close_reason: Implemented in PR #982: MutationDiff.create_filter (LLM-authored full filter JSON, operator-chosen approach) lets the experiment writer ADD a filter when base.filter is None. applicable_mutation_kinds offers filter on filterless strategies; shared filter_create_directive invites creation in both single-shot + tournament prompts; validate_filter_create validates via authoring::parse_filter_value -> xvision_filters::validate (invalid_filter_create on a clean retry); apply_to installs the filter + flips activation to FilterGated (never clobbers an existing one); committed via apply_to on both cycle paths. TDD; engine lib 1365 passed.

title: Fix deploy-image cleanup for Docker ps format compatibility
description: During a local-image deployment on 2026-06-06, scripts/deploy-image.sh loaded the new image successfully but its post-load cleanup emitted: failed to execute template at <.ImageID>: can't evaluate field ImageID in type *formatter.ContainerContext. The script then attempted to remove xvision:deploy-beaf0e6 and Docker correctly refused because xvnej-app still referenced the old image. The cleanup logic should use a Docker CLI-compatible way to detect container image IDs across installed Docker versions before selecting old xvision tags for removal.
acceptance_criteria: Post-load cleanup identifies containers referencing the prior image without using unsupported docker ps template fields; cleanup skips tags still referenced by any container; deploy-image.sh completes without cleanup template errors on root@100.120.48.1's Docker version.
close_reason: DONE: scripts/deploy-image.sh now uses {{.ImageID}} with 'docker ps -a' and {{.ID}} with 'docker images' (correct contexts); fix in commit 8fd7d482. Verified 2026-06-12 audit.
